senses.definition: to swallow something again or before another action, often to preempt the need for swallowing in a procedure or to prepare for ingestion
examples: The doctor asked her to preswallow the pill so it would start dissolving early.
They preswallow the liquid to check for a reaction before giving the full dose.

senses.definition: to add sweetness beforehand or to adjust sweetness prior to a process or tasting
examples: They presweeten the sauce before simmering to achieve a balanced flavor.
Chefs may presweeten a glaze to ensure the final dish is not bland.

senses.definition: already sweetened before use or consumption
examples: Presweetened yogurt is convenient for quick breakfasts.
Choose presweetened cereal if you want less sugar added later.

senses.definition: the act or process of adding sugar before use
examples: Presweetening can affect texture in baked goods.
The recipe calls for presweetening before folding in the batter.

senses.definition: third person singular form of presweeten; adds sweetness beforehand
examples: It presweetens the sauce to balance acidity.
The recipe presweetens the glaze for a richer finish.

senses.definition: situated in front of or near the sylvian region (anatomical term around the Sylvian fissure processing areas) or relating to the presylvian area of the brain
examples: Presylvian regions are involved in early stages of auditory processing.
The study mapped presylvian connectivity in the cortex.

senses.definition: to feel or understand someone else's feelings before fully experiencing them; to anticipate another's emotional state
examples: She could presympathize with his frustration after hearing the news.
The counselor presympathized with the family’s worries during the interview.

senses.definition: past tense of presympathize; understood or felt someone else's feelings in advance
examples: She presympathized with him before the meeting.
The team presympathized with the client’s concerns earlier.

senses.definition: present participle of presympathize; feeling or understanding someone else’s emotions in advance
examples: She is presympathizing with the affected families.
Presympathizing can help in de-escalating tense conversations.

senses.definition: feeling of sympathy or understanding in advance; a pre-emptive sense of compassion
examples: Her presympathy helped ease the conversation.
There is a note of presympathy in his response to the news.

senses.definition: related to music that precedes or prepares for a symphonic work; or reminiscent of a pre-symphony style
examples: The concert featured presymphonic movements that lit the mood.
critics described the piece as presymphonic in its grand build.

senses.definition: a work or portion of music that precedes a symphonic composition; an introductory or pre-symphonic piece
examples: The overture serves as a presymphony to the main symphony.
The program opened with a presymphony that set the stage.

senses.definition: situated in front of the symphysis (a joint) in anatomy; relating to the area before the symphysis
examples: The presymphysial region is examined in the study of pelvic anatomy.
Presymphysial landmarks help surgeons identify positions.

senses.definition: a symptom that appears before the main set of symptoms; an early warning sign
examples: Fatigue can be a presymptom of an underlying condition.
The doctor noted presymptoms before the outbreak worsened.

senses.definition: existing or occurring before symptoms appear, especially in diseases
examples: The patient was presymptomatic but contagious.
Presymptomatic transmission can complicate containment efforts.

senses.definition: the functional junction between two neurons, before the synapse forms or before neurotransmission occurs
examples: Presynapsis is involved in the alignment of nerve endings.
Researchers studied presynapsis to understand signal initiation.

senses.definition: before the synapse; relating to the neuron ending that releases neurotransmitters
examples: Presynaptic terminals release neurotransmitters.
Presynaptic machinery controls signal speed.

senses.definition: in a presynaptic manner; with respect to the presynaptic part of a neuron
examples: Signals are transmitted presynaptically before crossing the gap.
The protein acts presynaptically to regulate release.

senses.definition: relating to the area in front of the sacrum (lower back bone) near the spine
examples: Presynsacral measurements are used in anatomical studies.
Presynsacral approaches may be described in surgical texts.

senses.definition: before the system is organized or structured; not yet systematic
examples: The early phase of the project was presystematic and exploratory.
Presystematic notes were later reorganized into a formal plan.

senses.definition: in a presystematic manner; prior to forming a systematic approach
examples: The notes were presystematically collected before analysis.
Presystematically, researchers gathered diverse observations.

senses.definition: the interval just before a heartbeat's systole when the heart chambers fill
examples: Presystole is observed as the heart prepares to contract.
The ECG pattern shows a brief presystole phase.

senses.definition: occurring before systole; relating to the presystolic phase
examples: Presystolic murmurs can indicate valve issues.
The presystolic period lasts a fraction of a second.

senses.definition: a short form for a pretender or an easy pretense; also used as a slang or informal abbreviation in some contexts
examples: In some dialects, pret can mean a small trick or feint.
The rapper used 'pret' as a playful term in lyrics.

senses.definition: in some contexts a name or shorthand; not a widely used English word outside specific jargon
examples: In the dataset, preta appears as a label.
The term preta is not common in everyday English.
